subject: Social Science
chapter_name: Social and Political Life – II: Understanding Media
overall_learning_objectives: Know different types of media and sources of information
Understand how news is reported and shared
Learn about the role of media in society
Identify fake news and the importance of verifying information
Develop observation, analysis, and communication skills

lesson_plan.title: What is Media?
topics_covered: Types of media: print, broadcast, digital
teaching_strategy: Show pictures of newspapers, TV, and social media
Ask students what they use to get news
student_activities: Share one way media helps them

lesson_plan.title: News and Information
topics_covered: Different sections of newspapers or news portals
teaching_strategy: Show newspaper front page or online news portal
student_activities: Identify local, national, and sports news in a newspaper
Share which news interests them most and why

lesson_plan.title: Role of Media in Society
topics_covered: Media in education and social awareness
Media as a watchdog in society
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on recent news events
Use examples of media influencing positive change
student_activities: Give an example of a news story that helped society

lesson_plan.title: Fake News and Verification
topics_covered: How to verify news and information
teaching_strategy: Show a real vs fake news example
Discuss steps to verify online information
student_activities: Identify if a sample news story is true or false
Discuss how they can check facts

lesson_plan.title: Creating a Mini News Report
teaching_strategy: Demonstrate writing a short news snippet
Show examples of simple news reporting
student_activities: Write a 3–4 line news story on school or local event

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Quiz
topics_covered: Recap of types of media, news reporting, and verification
Relate media lessons to daily life
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with examples and multiple-choice questions
Class discussion connecting to their own media use
student_activities: Share one interesting thing learned about media

teaching_aids: Pictures of newspapers, TV, social media icons
Notebook and pen for writing activities
teacher_tip: Keep lessons interactive with real-life examples. Use newspapers, online news snippets, and discussions to make media relatable. Encourage students to ask questions and check facts themselves.
